title: Spanish Investors Achieve E-2 Visa Success with Eco-Friendly Business
content:
We are excited to share that Spanish investors continue to find success pursuing the American dream through investment. One of our clients, a Spanish investor, has received E-2 visa approval through a Change of Status. This milestone enables him to legally establish and operate a sanitation and disinfection company in Texas, highlighting how Spanish investors can leverage the E-2 visa to build businesses in the United States.

## Background of the Investor and Business Formation
The investor is a dual citizen of Spain and Venezuela. He has established full ownership (100%) of a Texas-based limited liability company (LLC) dedicated to sanitation and eco-conscious disinfection services. The company was legally incorporated in 2020 and received its Employer Identification Number (EIN) from the Internal Revenue Service (IRS), fulfilling one of the key E-2 visa eligibility requirements.
This case underscores how Spanish investors can create compliant U.S. business entities and fulfill the criteria required for E-2 visa issuance.

## E-2 Visa Business Plan and Operations in Texas
The newly formed sanitation company will operate in Spring and The Woodlands, Texas, delivering specialized cleaning services that prioritize environmental responsibility. The business uses EPA-approved disinfectants and a proprietary microbial blend that naturally digests fats, oils, and grease in drain systems. By offering an eco-friendly business in Texas, the investor supports both public health and the natural ecosystem through sustainable sanitation solutions.
Through innovative sanitation solutions, Spanish investors like our client contribute directly to community well-being and environmental preservation.

## Services and Market Reach
The business is strategically positioned to serve a wide range of commercial clients, including:
- George Bush Intercontinental Airport in Houston
Beyond on-site services, the company will also offer cleaning and sanitation products for both business and home use. This business model demonstrates how Spanish investors can diversify revenue streams and scale operations in the competitive U.S. market.

## E-2 Visa Requirements and Business Readiness
Our client’s E-2 visa business plan clearly outlined his investment strategy, operational model, and projected economic impact. This E-2 business met all critical treaty investor requirements:
- Substantial investment
- Full ownership by the Spanish treaty investor
- Direct operational control
The company has secured a commercial business location in Texas and is fully equipped to launch operations upon the investor’s arrival. He will assume the role of Owner and General Manager, overseeing day-to-day operations and business growth.
This case serves as a model for how Spanish investors can successfully transition to U.S. business ownership through the E-2 visa process.

## Wondering if You Qualify?
Find out if the E-2 visa is right for you. Get a free profile evaluation today.

## E-2 Visa for Spanish Investors: A Pathway to U.S. Business Ownership
The E-2 visa offers a powerful immigration pathway for Spanish investors who wish to start a business in the U.S. This successful E-2 approval highlights how the investment visa USA program helps qualified Spanish investors bring their entrepreneurial vision to life and contribute directly to the U.S. economy through job creation, innovation, and community-focused services.
Congratulations to our client on his E-2 visa approval. We look forward to supporting his journey and watching his sanitation business thrive in Texas.
